summary:
The fourth-generation apex locator uses multiple frequency ratios for enhanced detection capability by differentiating tissue properties accurately; this method surpasses basic resistive methods due to its ability to handle finer distinctions necessary for improved diagnostics. Agent1 reasoned that among provided parameters, using multiple frequencies offered superior sensitivity needed for modern apical locating technology aiming high precision through intricate analysis rather than relying solely on simple metrics such as pure resistance which lacks sufficient complexity. This summary captures both core aspects—the use of multifrequency techniques and their superiority compared to single-parameter approaches—in concise form aligned closely with original content while condensing into two clear statements focused directly upon clinically significant details pertinent to evaluating these devices' performance improvements. \(\blacksquare\) To summarize:

The new apex locator employs multiple frequency ratios for highly sensitive detection, offering better discrimination between tissues crucially enhancing accuracy beyond traditional methods focusing merely on resistance. These multi-frequency analyses provide deeper insight enabling precise location determinations vital for effective endodontic procedures.\( \square \) Summary focuses precisely on how multichannel
